steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selection list.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Select an option" dropdown within the Teams Capability popup in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the VS Code window to choose the project root directory.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: python

```python
import json
import os

manifest_path = '/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_ve3aC/appPackage/manifest.json'
new_version = '2.0.0'

if not os.path.exists(manifest_path):
    print(f"ERROR: File {manifest_path} does not exist.")
    exit(1)

with open(manifest_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as file:
    data = json.load(file)

data['version'] = new_version

with open(manifest_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as file:
    json.dump(data, file, indent=4)

print(f"Set 'version' to '{new_version}' in the manifest.json file.")
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Set 'version' to '2.0.0' in the manifest.json file.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code open file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/manifest.json, set value for \"version\" to a new value \"2.0.0\"
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.

Type 'Microsoft 365 Agents:update app' into the Visual Studio Code command palette to trigger the Microsoft 365 Agents extension update command, as indicated by the command palette dropdown and highlighted result within the VS Code interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Update App" command in the VS Code command palette to initiate the app update process within the Microsoft 365 Agents development environment.

Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the file selection dropdown within the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog of Visual Studio Code, targeting the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_jdmqM/appPackage".
Click on the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown menu at the top of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Preview and update" button in the manifest file configuration dialog within Visual Studio Code to confirm regenerating the manifest file and updating to the Teams platform.
@assertion  \"View in Developer Portal\" blue button  exist.
Click the **"View in Developer Portal"** button within the notification popup in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit, confirming manifest deployment success.
Click the ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}  account option in the Microsoft login dialog within Google Chrome, selecting the highlighted account from the "Pick an account" list.
Type the text ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}  into the password input field on the Microsoft login page for the account ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} in Google Chrome.

Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page in Google Chrome after entering the password. The interaction is highlighted by a red crosshair centered over the blue "Sign in" button below the password input field.
Click the "No" button on the "Stay signed in?" Microsoft login prompt in Google Chrome at login.microsoftonline.com, confirming the selection at the red crosshair location.
Click the "manifest.json" file name in the file list within the App package editor of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal to select or open the manifest file for editing.

Click the edit field for the "version" property in the manifest.json file within the App package editor of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal, placing the cursor after the first character of the version string (`"1"` in `"1.0.0"`).
Type text: '3' into the manifest.json code editor within the "App package editor" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application.

Click the left mouse button on the scroll bar in the manifest.json editor within the "App package editor" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal, using Google Chrome. The crosshair at (787, 756) is positioned over the vertical scroll bar, enabling scrolling through the JSON manifest file.

Click the "Save" button in the App package editor section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface. The button is located in the top-right area of the editor pane, highlighted with a blue background and white text.
Click the blue "Update" button in the "App package editor" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal to validate and save locally made changes to the app package.
Click the "Confirm" button in the "Review changes" dialog to acknowledge modifications to the manifest.json file within the App package editor on the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application.
Click the "Close" button (X) at the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window in the Developer Portal interface for Microsoft Teams, closing the "App package editor" view.
Press the F1 key in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette while viewing and editing the manifest.dev.json file within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Type the text "Microsoft 365 Agents:update app" into the Visual Studio Code command palette to invoke a specific Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit command within the development environment.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Update App" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate an app update within the Microsoft 365 development extension.

Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the file selection dropdown at the top of the "Select Teams manifest_json File" dialog within Visual Studio Code, highlighting the file path "/home/vsco/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_vk3Ee/appPackage/manifest.json".
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion   \"Overwrite and update\" exist.
